title: uragoga chinantlensis
intro: Uragoga chinantlensis, also known as wild yam, is a perennial plant native to Central America and Mexico, belonging to the Dioscoreaceae family. Traditionally, Uragoga chinantlensis has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The plant's health benefits include alleviating menstrual cramps, menopausal symptoms, and digestive issues, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. Uragoga chinantlensis is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or supplement, and can be found in various herbal preparations to support women's health and wellness.
